summary: Review of changes addressing berendt's external review #2 feedback. All 6 warnings and 3 info items were correctly addressed: parallel changed from 2 to 1, CI Trigger Policy marked as Planned with future tense, diagnostics matrix table updated to reflect actual coverage, and Keystone CR status diagnostic consistency addressed via shared diagnostics.sh (with REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comments explaining the architectural choice over inline kubectl commands). Review patterns documented for future reviews.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and follow established chainsaw patterns
Each scenario has unique CR name and database (no conflicts)
Catch blocks provide uniform diagnostics via diagnostics.sh
Parallel: 1 prevents cross-test interference with shared infrastructure

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing chainsaw E2E test patterns (SPDX, step comments, JMESPath)
DRY: diagnostics.sh eliminates duplicated catch block logic
Clear naming (test names, CR names, scenario IDs)
No dead code or commented-out code (excluding REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT annotations)

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in test fixtures
No sensitive data exposed in diagnostic output

architecture_checklist.item: Shared diagnostics.sh is simpler than duplicated inline scripts
Separate chainsaw-config.yaml for chaos tests with appropriate settings
File layout matches documented structure in docs/reference

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated catch block logic (consolidated in diagnostics.sh)

defensive_checklist.item: diagnostics.sh commands use '|| true' to prevent cascade failures
Unknown options in diagnostics.sh cause explicit exit 1

issues_found.description: REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comments contain German text 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen' (German for 'Reviewer: please verify'). Project rule requires all output in English.
issues_found.fix: Replace 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' with 'Reviewer: please verify.' in all 4 occurrences (mariadb Steps 4 and 6, openbao Steps 4 and 6).

issues_found.description: REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comments reference diagnostics.sh by line number '(line 52-53)' which will become stale when the script is modified.
issues_found.fix: Replace '(line 52-53)' with a description like '(Keystone CR status section)' that remains stable across edits.
issues_found.evidence: # already collects Keystone CR status (line 52-53) for both baseline and

suggested_improvements: Consider removing REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comments after berendt confirms the diagnostics.sh approach is acceptable — they serve as temporary review annotations but add noise to the codebase long-term.
next_steps: Await berendt's confirmation on the diagnostics.sh approach for Keystone CR status collection (REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT in mariadb-pod-kill and openbao-pod-kill Steps 4 and 6)
